meta.title: GCP-PDE-4: Dataflow Design, BigQuery Optimization, and Governed Operations

domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: A company needs durable asynchronous event ingestion with decoupled publishers and subscribers on Google Cloud. Which service is the best fit?

options.explanation: Pub/Sub is Google Cloud’s managed messaging service for event ingestion and decoupled consumers.

questions.stem: A solution must support the same business logic for streaming processing and historical backfills. Which technology is most aligned with that requirement?

options.explanation: Apache Beam on Dataflow provides a unified programming model for batch and streaming pipelines.

questions.stem: A team needs a globally distributed relational database with strong consistency and SQL support. Which service should it choose?

options.explanation: Cloud Spanner is the globally distributed relational database with SQL and strong consistency.

questions.stem: Which TWO considerations are most important when choosing between BigQuery and Bigtable? (Select TWO)

options.text: Whether the workload is analytical SQL or low-latency key-based access
options.explanation: BigQuery is optimized for analytical SQL, while Bigtable is optimized for low-latency key-based access patterns.

options.text: Expected query pattern and access latency requirements
options.explanation: BigQuery is optimized for analytical SQL, while Bigtable is optimized for low-latency key-based access patterns.

questions.stem: A retailer expects infrequent ad hoc analytics over large historical datasets with minimal infrastructure management. Which service is usually the best fit?

options.explanation: BigQuery is the serverless analytical warehouse for large-scale SQL analytics.

questions.stem: A team wants to minimize vendor lock-in in pipeline logic while still using a managed runner on Google Cloud. Which design choice best supports that goal?

options.text: Author pipelines in Apache Beam and run them on Dataflow
options.explanation: Beam provides a portable model, while Dataflow offers the managed execution environment.

options.text: Write every transform as a Cloud Scheduler cron job
Use only manual BigQuery console actions

questions.stem: Which feature of BigQuery most directly separates storage and compute from an architectural perspective?

options.text: Serverless managed execution that queries data stored in Colossus
options.explanation: BigQuery abstracts infrastructure and separates analytical compute from the underlying managed storage layer.

options.text: Need to create HDFS clusters first

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud service is commonly used to ingest application events for downstream streaming analytics?

options.explanation: Pub/Sub is the standard managed event ingestion service for many streaming architectures.

questions.stem: A Pub/Sub subscription should isolate poison messages after repeated delivery failures. Which feature should be configured?

options.explanation: Pub/Sub dead-letter topics route messages that exceed the configured delivery attempts.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about Dataflow streaming pipelines are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Windowing helps group unbounded data into logical chunks for computation.
options.explanation: Windowing and watermarks are core streaming concepts in Dataflow. Dataflow is managed and can write to BigQuery.

options.text: Watermarks help reason about event-time progress.
options.explanation: Windowing and watermarks are core streaming concepts in Dataflow. Dataflow is managed and can write to BigQuery.

options.text: Dataflow requires manual VM patching by the customer.
Streaming pipelines cannot write to BigQuery.

questions.stem: A team must process both late and on-time events correctly in event-time aggregations. Which concept is most relevant?

options.explanation: Watermarks help streaming systems reason about event-time completeness and late data handling.

questions.stem: A company needs a managed Spark and Hadoop environment with cluster-level configuration control and optional autoscaling. Which service best fits?

options.explanation: Dataproc is Google Cloud’s managed service for Spark, Hadoop, and related open-source data frameworks.

questions.stem: Which service is best suited for scheduled orchestration of complex Google Cloud data workflows with dependencies across tasks?

options.explanation: Cloud Composer is the managed Apache Airflow service for orchestrating multi-step workflows.

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataflow instead of Dataproc for many ETL workloads?

options.text: Dataflow is serverless and manages the execution environment for pipeline jobs
options.explanation: Dataflow reduces infrastructure management because it is a fully managed pipeline runner.

questions.stem: Which TWO ingestion patterns are commonly used for near-real-time analytics on Google Cloud? (Select TWO)

options.text: Publish events to Pub/Sub and process them with Dataflow
options.explanation: Pub/Sub plus Dataflow and CDC into analytical sinks are both common near-real-time patterns.

options.text: Write micro-batches directly from an app to a manually managed spreadsheet
Capture operational database changes and stream them into analytical sinks
options.explanation: Pub/Sub plus Dataflow and CDC into analytical sinks are both common near-real-time patterns.

options.text: Wait for yearly archive exports before all processing

questions.stem: A Dataflow batch job repeatedly reprocesses all source objects even though only new files should be handled. Which design adjustment is usually most appropriate?

options.text: Track processed inputs or land data in a way that supports incremental discovery
options.explanation: Incremental pipelines need a reliable notion of what is new, often via manifesting, partitioning, or tracked state.

options.text: Replace Cloud Storage with Cloud DNS
Store outputs in local laptop folders

questions.stem: Which BigQuery table design feature is most useful for pruning data in queries filtered by ingestion date or event date?

options.explanation: Partitioning reduces scanned data when filters align to the partition column.

questions.stem: When should BigQuery clustering typically be considered?

options.text: When commonly filtered or grouped columns can benefit from data colocation within partitions or tables
options.explanation: Clustering organizes storage based on chosen columns and can improve scan efficiency for selective queries.

options.text: Only when IAM policies are missing

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about Cloud Bigtable schema design are accurate? (Select TWO)

options.text: Row key design strongly affects access patterns and hotspot risk.
options.explanation: Bigtable performance depends heavily on row-key design and is aimed at low-latency NoSQL workloads.

options.text: Bigtable is well suited for high-throughput, low-latency key-based workloads.
options.explanation: Bigtable performance depends heavily on row-key design and is aimed at low-latency NoSQL workloads.

options.text: Bigtable is the default choice for ad hoc ANSI SQL warehousing.
Bigtable automatically normalizes all entities into star schemas.

questions.stem: A team wants objects older than 180 days to transition automatically to a colder and cheaper storage class. Which Cloud Storage capability should it use?

options.explanation: Cloud Storage lifecycle rules automate transitions and deletion based on object age and conditions.

questions.stem: A data engineer needs sub-10-millisecond lookups for user profiles keyed by user ID at massive scale. Which store is the best fit?

options.explanation: Bigtable is designed for very high-throughput, low-latency key-based access.

questions.stem: Which service is primarily an analytical data warehouse rather than a NoSQL key-value store?

options.explanation: Cloud Storage lifecycle rules automate transitions and deletions based on age and other object conditions, which fits this retention requirement.

questions.stem: Which TWO practices generally improve long-term analytical storage efficiency in BigQuery? (Select TWO)

options.text: Use partitioning that matches common filter predicates
options.explanation: Appropriate partitioning and practical table design reduce scan cost and improve manageability.

options.text: Avoid storing everything in one huge unpartitioned table when filters are predictable
options.explanation: Appropriate partitioning and practical table design reduce scan cost and improve manageability.

options.text: Add random columns with no analytical purpose to every table
Prefer tiny fragmented schemas solely to increase table count

questions.stem: A team needs a storage layer that can preserve raw files in open formats before loading them elsewhere. Which service is the natural choice?

options.explanation: Cloud Storage is the standard raw object storage layer in many Google Cloud data architectures.

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature can precompute and incrementally maintain results for certain repeated query patterns?

options.explanation: Materialized views can improve performance for repeated aggregations or query patterns that meet BigQuery requirements.

questions.stem: A security team wants to restrict analysts so they can see only rows for their assigned region in a BigQuery table. Which feature most directly fits?

options.explanation: BigQuery row-level security applies filters so users only see allowed rows.

questions.stem: Why are authorized views useful in BigQuery?

options.text: They can expose a controlled subset of data without granting access to all base tables
options.explanation: Authorized views support governed data sharing patterns over underlying tables.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about analytical modeling in BigQuery are generally correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Denormalization is often used to reduce costly joins for some analytics workloads.
options.explanation: Analytical schema design should align with workload patterns, and BigQuery supports richer structures than plain CSV.

options.text: Schema choices should reflect query patterns and governance needs.
options.explanation: Analytical schema design should align with workload patterns, and BigQuery supports richer structures than plain CSV.

options.text: Tables cannot contain nested or repeated fields.

questions.stem: A company wants centralized governance, discovery, and data quality management across lakes, warehouses, and metadata on Google Cloud. Which service is most aligned?

options.explanation: Dataplex provides a governance layer for discovery, quality, and management across distributed data assets.

questions.stem: Which service is primarily used for SQL-based analytics and BI over large datasets?

options.explanation: BigQuery is the core managed SQL analytics warehouse on Google Cloud and is the standard choice for large-scale BI and ad hoc analytics.

questions.stem: A team runs the same dashboard query pattern repeatedly and wants predictable performance isolation for business-critical reporting. Which BigQuery concept is most relevant?

options.explanation: BigQuery reservations and slots help allocate dedicated query capacity for predictable workloads.

questions.stem: Which TWO outcomes are common reasons to use policy-based governance features in analytical systems? (Select TWO)

options.text: Restrict visibility of sensitive data to the right audience
options.explanation: Governance controls aim to protect sensitive data and make sharing safer and more scalable.

options.text: Support compliant sharing patterns without duplicating every dataset manually
options.explanation: Governance controls aim to protect sensitive data and make sharing safer and more scalable.

questions.stem: Which service is commonly used to orchestrate scheduled DAG-based data workflows on Google Cloud?

options.explanation: Cloud Composer is the managed Airflow service for workflow orchestration.

questions.stem: A Dataflow pipeline needs observability for throughput, errors, and lag so operators can respond quickly. What should the team prioritize?

options.explanation: Operational monitoring and alerting are key for managed pipeline reliability.

questions.stem: Why might a team choose Dataproc autoscaling for a batch Spark environment?

options.text: To adjust cluster resources based on workload demand
options.explanation: Dataproc autoscaling helps align cluster resources with workload needs and cost targets.

options.text: To convert BigQuery SQL into spreadsheets
To remove IAM from the project
To eliminate the need for storage

questions.stem: Which TWO activities are part of reliable data workload operations? (Select TWO)

options.explanation: Reliable operations depend on observability and repeatable deployment practices.

options.text: Using infrastructure automation and repeatable deployments
options.explanation: Reliable operations depend on observability and repeatable deployment practices.

questions.stem: A team wants to reduce surprise BigQuery costs from unconstrained ad hoc queries. Which control is most directly relevant?

options.text: Capacity planning plus query governance such as reservations or cost monitoring
options.explanation: Monitoring, workload management, and capacity controls help manage BigQuery spend.

options.text: Moving all data to local drives

questions.stem: Which Google Cloud product is the managed monitoring and alerting platform?

options.explanation: Cloud Monitoring provides metrics, dashboards, and alerting.

questions.stem: What is a strong reason to keep pipeline definitions in source control and automate deployments?

options.text: It improves reproducibility, review, and consistent environment promotion
options.explanation: Version-controlled automation improves consistency and change management across environments.

options.text: It guarantees queries are always free
It removes the need for IAM

questions.stem: A pipeline owner needs to rerun a failed backfill safely after fixing code. Which design principle helps most?

options.text: Idempotent writes and clearly scoped input ranges
options.explanation: Idempotent writes and bounded rerun scopes make recovery safer and more predictable.

options.text: Randomly changing destination table names each run
Mixing production and test outputs in one path
